<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what DOWN changed
before = last_transition.before_frame
after = last_transition.after_frame

        if before_lines[r][c] != after_lines[r][c]:
            changed.append((r, c, before_lines[r][c], after_lines[r][c]))
print("DOWN changed:", changed)
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.content:
DOWN also only changed the HUD bar. So UP/DOWN only affect the HUD bar, not the game state.

- SPACE: Splits the white block, turning top half purple (only meaningful gameplay action so far)
- LEFT: Rotates container+block into diagonal
- RIGHT: Undoes LEFT
- UP/DOWN: Only change HUD bar
- MOUSE on game objects: No impact
I'm running out of obvious actions to try. Let me reconsider the problem. Maybe the game isn't about matching the reference panel. Maybe SPACE is the key action and I need to use it multiple times, or in combination with LEFT/RIGHT.
Let me try SPACE again to see what happens now.
